macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Trump's Economy Approval Rating Hits New Career Low of 31%
items.summary: President Trump's approval rating for handling the economy has fallen to a new career low of 31% according to a CNN/SSRS poll, with roughly two-thirds of Americans saying his policies have worsened economic conditions. Only 27% approve of his handling of inflation, down from 44% one year ago.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge 50-59% Following Iran Military Strikes and Strait of Hormuz Closure
items.summary: Brent crude oil has surged 50% and WTI prices up roughly 59% since military strikes began on February 27th, as the Strait of Hormuz closure effectively shuts off 20% of the world's oil supply. This marks the fourth major economic shock since 2019.

items.title: Stock Market Rallies on Unconfirmed Reports of Iran War Deal
items.summary: Markets are rallying amid unconfirmed reports of a potential deal to end the war with Iran, providing relief from recent economic pressures as Americans experience rising gas prices at the pump.

items.title: Core Inflation Remains Above Federal Reserve Target Amid Energy Shock
items.summary: Core CPI inflation is running at 3.0% on a three-month annualized basis and core PCE at 3.7%, both above the Federal Reserve's target, even before the recent Iran conflict escalated energy prices.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $68,000 Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Signals and Trump Comments
items.summary: Bitcoin rose to around $68,500, up 1.5-3.1% in the past 24 hours, driven by de-escalation in US-Iran tensions, Trump withdrawal signals, falling oil prices, and VIX decline boosting risk assets. Crypto market cap increased 1.3% to $2.35T-$2.41T with Bitcoin ETFs seeing $117M inflows and $166M short liquidations.

items.title: Blur (BLUR) Leads Crypto Gainers with 25% Surge
items.summary: Blur (BLUR) topped gainers with a 25.46% increase to $0.0217 on $17.71M volume, followed by Arcblock (ABT) at 3.64%, amid overall market rebound. Other notable gainers include Gravity (G) up 2.26% on high $122M volume.

items.title: Algorand Surges 22% as Top Performer in Recovering Market
items.summary: Algorand gained over 22% leading the market rebound, while Zcash and Neo rose 4-7%; TRON, Bitcoin Cash, and Toncoin saw minor losses under 1.5%. Fear and Greed Index remains at extreme fear level of 8.

items.title: Ethereum Recovers Above $2,100 with 3-4% Gains
items.summary: Ethereum climbed back above $2,100-$2,135, up nearly 4% in 24 hours, outperforming alongside altcoins amid broader market recovery tied to geopolitical easing.

items.title: Quantum Computing Threat to Bitcoin Highlighted by Google Analysts
items.summary: Google analysts warn quantum computers could hack Bitcoin sooner than expected, potentially requiring less power and endangering nearly a third of existing BTC.

items.title: Nakamoto Sells 284 BTC at 40% Loss Amid Share Plunge
items.summary: Nakamoto, led by Trump's former crypto adviser David Bailey, sold 284 BTC at a 40% loss as its shares dropped 99% over the past year.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: UK and Canada Sign AI Compute Agreement
items.summary: The UK and Canada signed a landmark agreement on April 1, 2026, to collaborate on computing power for advancing AI research and development.

items.title: DeepL Report: 83% of Enterprises Lag in Language AI Adoption
items.summary: DeepL's 2026 Borderless Business report, released April 1, 2026, reveals 83% of enterprises have not modernized language workflows, emphasizing language AI as essential for global growth.

items.title: UCLA Study: AI Lacks Fundamental Human Cognitive Property
items.summary: A UCLA Health study published April 1, 2026, argues that current AI systems miss bodily interaction and internal state awareness, impacting their safety and trustworthiness.

items.title: Security Challenges Emerge with Agentic AI Systems
items.summary: New analysis highlights risks in agentic AI like memory poisoning and trifecta vulnerabilities (private data access, untrusted content, external communication), with only 13% of professionals prepared per ISACA's 2026 survey.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Sparks Concern Over Destiny 2's Future with Recent Updates
items.summary: Bungie's latest communication has raised worries about Destiny 2, with speculation on whether they'll release Destiny 3, continue content updates, or scale back amid delays until June.

items.title: Destiny 2 Weekly Reset Reveals Hidden Modifiers and Free Competitive Weapon
items.summary: New Destiny 2: Renegades update during Guardian Games 2026 introduces hidden portal event modifiers and the Peculiar Charm as a free competitive PvP weapon from Zavala.

items.title: Fortnite Launches 24-Hour April Fool's Update with Free Rewards and Silly Features
items.summary: Fortnite's limited-time update includes stackable players, big heads, free rewards, and teases upcoming Toy Story skins like Woody and Buzz, available only for the next 24 hours.

items.title: Gaming Industry Faces Price Hikes from PlayStation, Nintendo, and Sony's PC Port Halt
items.summary: PlayStation raises prices, Nintendo adds $10 premium to physical games over digital, and Sony announces no more PC ports, signaling a trend of making gaming less accessible.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Agrees to $14.2B Buyback of Apollo's Fab 34 Stake in Ireland
items.summary: Intel will repurchase Apollo's 49% stake in the Fab 34 joint venture in Ireland for $14.2 billion, planning to issue $6.5B in new debt to fund the acquisition. The stock rose 10% on the announcement.

items.title: AT&T Secures $2B Deal to Upgrade FirstNet Emergency Network
items.summary: AT&T agreed to a deal worth up to $2 billion to upgrade the FirstNet emergency cellular network it operates for the US Commerce Department.

items.title: Raspberry Pi Raises Prices Citing Higher Memory Costs
items.summary: Raspberry Pi increased prices by between $11.25 and $150 following December and February hikes, and unveiled a new 3GB Raspberry Pi 4 model priced at $83.75.

items.title: Baidu Robotaxis Malfunction Across Wuhan
items.summary: A suspected system failure froze Baidu's robotaxis across Wuhan, trapping passengers and reportedly causing traffic disruptions and crashes.

items.title: Mercor Hit by Supply Chain Attack via LiteLLM
items.summary: AI recruiting startup Mercor confirmed a security incident linked to a supply chain attack involving the open-source project LiteLLM, with hacker group Lapsus$ claiming to have accessed and stolen company data.
